name: Build Releases with GoReleaser
description: Automate Go binary releases across platforms with GoReleaser, including changelogs, checksums, and GitHub releases.
trendingReason: GoReleaser is the standard tool for publishing Go project releases

steps.title: Install GoReleaser
steps.description: Install GoReleaser using Go install or your package manager.
steps.tip: On macOS you can also use: brew install goreleaser

steps.title: Customize the configuration
steps.description: Edit .goreleaser.yaml to define build targets, archives, and changelog settings.
steps.command:
cat > .goreleaser.yaml << 'EOF'
version: 2
builds:
  - main: ./cmd/myapp
    binary: myapp
    env:
      - CGO_ENABLED=0
    goos:
      - linux
      - darwin
      - windows
    goarch:
      - amd64
      - arm64
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -X main.version={{.Version}}

archives:
  - format: tar.gz
    name_template: "{{ .ProjectName }}_{{ .Os }}_{{ .Arch }}"
    format_overrides:
      - goos: windows
        format: zip

checksum:
  name_template: checksums.txt

changelog:
  sort: asc
  filters:
    exclude:
      - '^docs:'
      - '^chore:'
EOF

steps.title: Test the release locally
steps.description: Run a snapshot build without publishing to verify everything works.
steps.tip: Check the dist/ directory for the generated artifacts.

steps.title: Create a tag and publish the release
steps.description: Tag a version in Git and run GoReleaser to publish to GitHub.
steps.command: git tag -a v1.0.0 -m 'Release v1.0.0' && git push origin v1.0.0 && goreleaser release --clean
steps.warning: Make sure your GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable is set with repo permissions.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# GoReleaser Automation
set -euo pipefail

VERSION=${1:?"Usage: $0 <version> [--snapshot]"}
SNAPSHOT=${2:-""}

echo "=== GoReleaser Version ==="
goreleaser --version

if [ "$SNAPSHOT" = "--snapshot" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "=== Snapshot Build (no publish) ==="
  goreleaser release --snapshot --clean
else
  echo ""
  echo "=== Creating Git Tag ==="
  git tag -a "$VERSION" -m "Release $VERSION"
  git push origin "$VERSION"

  echo ""
  echo "=== Publishing Release ==="
  goreleaser release --clean
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Build Artifacts ==="
ls -lh dist/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No dist/ directory found"

echo ""
echo "=== GoReleaser Commands ==="
echo "Init:     goreleaser init"
echo "Check:    goreleaser check"
echo "Snapshot: goreleaser release --snapshot --clean"
echo "Release:  goreleaser release --clean"

faq.question: How do I integrate GoReleaser with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Use the official goreleaser/goreleaser-action. Trigger it on tag pushes. Set the GITHUB_TOKEN secret and GoReleaser handles the rest.

faq.question: Can GoReleaser build Docker images too?
faq.answer: Yes. Add a 'dockers' section to .goreleaser.yaml to build and push Docker images as part of the release process.

faq.question: How do I generate a changelog from conventional commits?
faq.answer: Configure the changelog section with groups that match commit prefixes like 'feat:', 'fix:', 'docs:'. GoReleaser will categorize changelog entries automatically.
